name: FASTENAL CO
business: Fastenal distributes industrial and construction supplies, including fasteners, safety products, cutting tools, and other MRO items. Its primary customers are in manufacturing and non-residential construction. The company operates a network of branches and utilizes technologies like vending and bin stock.
sellsTo: Manufacturing (heavy machinery, fabricated products, process industries, transportation components including aerospace and automotive)
Transportation services (air, rail, truck, warehousing, fulfillment centers)
Government and education (state, local, schools, universities)

exposures.sentence: We currently operate 12 of our North American distribution centers with automated storage and retrieval systems (ASRS).

infrared (IR) that uses IR sensors lining individual bins to provide real-time visibility of approximate quantity and inventory values, notifying Fastenal to replenish when inventory hits an established minimum threshold;
eCommerce (transactional website sales), which provide a means for our customers to effectively and efficiently procure indirect and unplanned spend.

Cybersecurity incidents, or violations of data privacy laws and regulations, could cause us to experience certain operational interruptions, incur substantial additional costs, become subject to legal or regulatory proceedings, or suffer damage to our reputation in the marketplace.

We also service general and commercial contractors in non-residential end markets as well as farmers, truckers, railroads, oil exploration companies, oil production and refinement companies, mining companies, federal, state, and local government entities, schools, warehouse and storage, data centers, and certain retail trades.

Approximately 71% to 76% of our customers are in manufacturing end markets, which encompass heavy machinery, fabricated products, process industries (oil & gas, petrochemical, mining, pulp and paper, etc.), and transportation components (automotive, aerospace, etc.).

Other applications are assisted by artificial intelligence (AI) to analyze customer usage data to recommend optimized parts and quantity for specific devices, which improves customer inventories while reducing the risk of stock-outs.

We transport product between our distribution centers and from our distribution centers to our selling locations. We typically transport approximately 90% of our products on our own fleet of Class 6, 7, and 8 trucks.

catalysts.event: Achieve 66% of sales volume through Digital Footprint
catalysts.sentence: We expect that during 2026 we will achieve 66% of our sales volume running through our Digital Footprint.

catalysts.event: Capital expenditure for property and equipment within $310M-$330M
catalysts.sentence: For 2026, we expect our investment in property and equipment, net of proceeds from sales, to be within a range of $310.0 to $330.0, an increase from $230.6 in 2025.

catalysts.event: Replace Atlanta hub facility to improve picking capacity
catalysts.sentence: First, we expect increased spending to replace our Atlanta hub facility and improve our picking capacity and efficiency across our hub network.

tamClaims.claim: North American marketplace for industrial supplies exceeds $140 billion per year
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the North American marketplace for industrial supplies is in excess of $140 billion per year

tamClaims.claim: Market could support up to 1.7 million industrial vending units
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the market could support as many as 1.7 million vending units

tamClaims.claim: Over 13,000 customer locations have potential for Onsite service model
tamClaims.sentence: Within North America, we believe the potential market opportunity for industrial vending is approximately 1.7 million devices and we have identified over 13,000 customer locations with the potential to implement our Onsite service model within our traditional manufacturing and construction customer base.
